name: session-scoped-stop-hook
args: Fix: the plugin-wide Stop hook (scripts/hooks/enforce-pipeline-progress.ts) discovers any active dp pipeline run under cwd's .claude/feature-pipeline/ without filtering by session. Two concurrent Claude Code sessions in the same project both see and act on each other's runs — one session's text-stop gets blocked by the other session's still-running pipeline, and the block message even suggests invoking the other session's currentStep skill. Need to scope hook discovery (and ideally run discovery in findActiveRun) to the session that owns the run, e.g. by tagging state.json with DEEP_SESSION_ID (or equivalent) at init time and filtering by it in findActiveRun and the Stop hook.
